## Definition of Done
Documentation is done when **all** of the following are satisfied:
 
- [ ] Documentation is written clearly and follows the project's style
- [ ] All code examples are accurate and tested
- [ ] Documentation is proofread for spelling and grammar
- [ ] All links are functional
- [ ] Screenshots/diagrams are up-to-date and clear
- [ ] Documentation is placed in the correct location
- [ ] Table of contents is updated if applicable
- [ ] Documentation has been reviewed and approved
- [ ] Changes are merged to the main branch
- [ ] Documentation is accessible and properly formatted (Markdown, etc.)
